key_insight: Joint MTP-RL training fails under fixed losses because the MTP effect splits into a helpful first-order correlation and a harmful second-order penalty; Optimal Coefficient Calibration (OCC) adaptively balances them to recover strong joint training.
engineering_relevance: For reasoning-model training pipelines, OCC offers a low-overhead way to keep multi-token prediction active without the performance drop seen in standard joint RL fine-tuning.
method: Decompose the MTP contribution to the RL objective into correlation and quadratic perturbation terms.
Unify and compare three regimes: Detach, Cross-Entropy loss, and Policy loss.
Track an online optimal coefficient using a log-probability proxy to calibrate MTP strength adaptively.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on six competition-level mathematical reasoning benchmarks, where OCC matches or exceeds the detach baseline.
watch_item: The calibration relies on an online proxy, so stability and transfer across model scales or reward setups may require careful tuning.
paper_title: Joint Training of Multi-Token Prediction in Reinforcement Learning via Optimal Coefficient Calibration
